entry_points.summary: Start from the native ATP daemon example.
Inject cancellation at deterministic await points.
Build an explicit-Cx pooled HTTP request with fluent headers, query, JSON body, timeout, and response helpers.
Exercise HTTP client pooling and protocol behavior.
Load and run YAML-backed deterministic lab scenarios.
Use two-phase MPSC send/receive paths in an end-to-end example.
Use deterministic node-local process-group names, member snapshots, event cursors, and broadcast accounting.
Expose metrics with the Prometheus example surface.
Race futures with loser drain semantics.
Run a deterministic RaptorQ transfer workflow.
Build a runtime and enter an async main body.
Own spawned work under a structured scope.
Build a minimal supervised application topology.
Convert handler panics into [ASUP-E502] 500 responses without killing the worker.
Negotiated response compression as a router layer.
Structured request/response logging with outcome severity, duration, and 499-on-cancel.
Stack middleware onion-style onto a router via the unified Layer trait.
Expose deterministic route metadata for API-surface, debug, and startup-report consumers.
Per-route timeouts, optionally tightened by the request budget (min-plus).

notes: Default contract compares root public exports from src/lib.rs for fast drift detection.
Pass --rustdoc-json to extend item-level extraction without changing the artifact contract.
